name: Squirrel Pot Pie
recipeInstructions:
DOUGH
2 cups flour
2/3 cup shortening
3/4 teaspoon salt
5 tablespoons water
1 egg
Cook the squirrels in a large pan, using enough water to cover. Salt to taste (about 1 tsp). Cook for 2-3 hours or until tender. Remove the cooked squirrels from the pan and cut into pieces. Roll out the dough on a floured surface, sprinkle with flour, and fry in shortening over medium heat until browned. Mix together all ingredients for the dough using a fork to incorporate the flour through other ingredients. Work gently with your hands for 1 minute. Roll out the dough on a floured surface to about 1/8 inch thickness. Cut into 1-inch or bite-sized pieces and add to boiling water with potatoes. Add 1 tablespoon parsley flakes and salt. If not enough salt is added, the pot pie will taste bland. Cook for 30 minutes at low heat.
